title: icmpv6: build_echo/build_error return type truncation risk
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/icmpv6.c
Lines: 251-310 (build_echo), 341-403 (build_error)

Both build_echo() and build_error() compute total_len as size_t and return it cast to int:
```c
return (int)total_len;
```

If total_len exceeds INT_MAX, this cast causes undefined behavior (signed integer overflow on most platforms) or truncation.

Analysis:
- For build_echo: total_len = 48 + data_len (40 IPv6 hdr + 8 ICMPv6 echo hdr + payload)
- For build_error: total_len = 48 + min(invoking_len, 256)

Mitigating factors:
- LICHEN_L2_MTU is 200 bytes, bounding practical packet sizes
- out_len bounds are checked before the cast
- Callers would need to pass malicious buffer sizes (SIZE_MAX or similar)

Severity: P4 (Low) - Theoretical issue unlikely to occur in practice due to MTU constraints, but defensive coding would add an explicit check:
```c
if (total_len > INT_MAX) {
    return -EOVERFLOW;
}
return (int)total_len;
```

Note: The return type is int (not size_t) to allow negative error codes. Consider documenting the maximum valid return value in the API.
notes: Commit: 132c299ef - added INT_MAX check and -EOVERFLOW returns in build_echo/build_error per bead
close_reason: Fixed: added if (total_len > (size_t)INT_MAX) return -EOVERFLOW; before casts in build_echo() and build_error() in lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/icmpv6.c (plus <limits.h>); follows project pattern from senml.c/oscore.c; tests blocked by pre-existing board.yml validation error but code change is isolated and correct. Commit 132c299ef
